sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
The fukui-reactivity analysis on aniline was performed using the chemkit tools. The calculation used Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and the def2-TZVP basis set (tier `standard`). The environment was the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The geometry was taken from the provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz` file. Partial charges were determined using the Mulliken scheme in PySCF.

The most electrophilic site (highest f+ value) is carbon atom 3 (index 2) with an f+ value of 2.2975867.
The most nucleophilic site (highest f- value) is nitrogen atom 1 (index 0) with an f- value of 0.20452359.

The calculation log was streamed live to a file named similar to `fukui-reactivity_<timestamp>.out` in the current working directory.
